title: Giving Set (Fluid Administration)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Administration of intravenous fluids and medications. 

#### Procedure

- Remove fluid from outer bag and check for discolouration or sedimentation.

- Confirm “5 rights” with paramedic partner, **the right**: 
- Patient
- Drug
- Dose
- Time
- Route
- Remove giving set from pack and reposition drip regulator near fluid chamber and turn off the roller regulator.

- Remove protective cap from both fluid bag and perforator, taking care not to contaminate either. Discard should contamination occur.

- Carefully insert perforator into fluid bag port taking care not to puncture the port wall. A small amount of fluid should enter the chamber.

- Suspend the bag and squeeze the lower half of the drip chamber until half full.

- Prime the line by opening the regulator allowing fluid to run. Ensure all air is removed from the line and needleless access port.

- Close the regulator roller when the line is full and no air bubbles remain.

- Remove protective cap and insert the end of the line into the cannula. Secure by tightening the rotating collar.

- Secure the IV line to the patient with tape, armlock, bandage, etc.

#### Specific Notes

- Control flow rate by toggling roller regulator.
- Regularly reassess for dislodgment, swelling, reddening or discomfort.
- Always ensure medication pre administration checks are completed prior to any drug administration.
